correct_procedure: Use the SONiC config CLI for all BGP configuration: 'config bgp neighbor add', 'config bgp neighbor remove', 'config bgp listen range', etc. For route-maps, prefix-lists, and other FRR policy objects that the SONiC CLI does not yet expose, add them to the bgpcfgd Jinja2 templates or use the 'config bgp device-global' and BGP_GLOBALS CONFIG_DB table entries. After making changes, run 'config save' to persist. If direct FRR debugging is needed (e.g., 'show bgp' for diagnosis), use 'docker exec bgp vtysh' in read-only mode: 'vtysh -c "show bgp summary"'. Never enter FRR configuration mode (conf t) via vtysh.

detection_commands.command: diff <(docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config' 2>/dev/null) <(docker exec bgp python3 /usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/bgpcfgd/bgpcfgd.py --print-config 2>/dev/null || sonic-cfggen -d -t /usr/share/sonic/templates/frr.conf.j2 2>/dev/null) | head -60
detection_commands.purpose: Diff the live FRR running config (what vtysh sees) against what bgpcfgd would generate from the current CONFIG_DB state. Lines present in FRR but absent from the generated config were added via direct vtysh and will be lost on the next bgpcfgd render.

detection_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config' 2>/dev/null | grep -vE '^(Building|Current|!|\s*$)' | sort > /tmp/frr_live.txt && sonic-cfggen -d -t /usr/share/sonic/templates/frr.conf.j2 2>/dev/null | grep -vE '^(Building|Current|!|\s*$)' | sort > /tmp/frr_expected.txt && diff /tmp/frr_live.txt /tmp/frr_expected.txt
detection_commands.purpose: Sorted diff to identify neighbor statements, route-maps, or prefix-lists present in the live FRR config but not in the CONFIG_DB-derived template. Each '< ' line is a direct-vtysh addition that will be lost on restart.
List all BGP neighbors known to CONFIG_DB. Compare against 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show bgp neighbors"' to find neighbors configured directly in FRR that have no CONFIG_DB entry.

detection_commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config' 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^\s*(neighbor|route-map|prefix-list|router bgp)' | sort
detection_commands.purpose: Extract the BGP-relevant lines from the live FRR config for manual comparison against CONFIG_DB BGP tables.
Check CONFIG_DB for route-map and prefix-list entries. Any route-map or prefix-list visible in 'vtysh -c show running-config' but absent here was configured directly in FRR.

Look for bgpcfgd log lines showing when it last generated and applied a FRR config template. The timestamp of the last 'Generating FRR config' entry is the point after which any vtysh changes would still be present -- a subsequent entry means they were overwritten.
Audit the system journal for evidence of direct vtysh invocations. May not catch all sessions but will capture invocations logged by the shell or audit daemon.

display_name: Direct FRR configuration via docker exec bgp vtysh bypassing bgpcfgd
pattern: Engineer connects directly to the FRR daemon inside the bgp container using 'docker exec -it bgp vtysh' and issues configuration commands (router bgp, neighbor, route-map, prefix-list, etc.) instead of using the SONiC config CLI or editing CONFIG_DB. Changes take effect immediately in FRR but are invisible to bgpcfgd and are not saved in CONFIG_DB.
prevention: Restrict shell access to the bgp container in production: remove 'docker' group membership from non-admin users, or configure sudoers to allow only read-only vtysh invocations ('vtysh -c "show ..."'). Add a bgpcfgd config-drift check to the monitoring stack: alert when the diff between live FRR config and the expected template output is non-empty.
symptoms: BGP neighbor comes up after vtysh change but drops after 'config reload' or bgp container restart with no config CLI change made
'show bgp summary' shows a neighbor or route-map that is absent from 'show running-config' (SONiC config CLI output)
After bgp container restart: 'show bgp summary' reverts to state matching CONFIG_DB, losing the vtysh-applied changes
Route-map or prefix-list applied in vtysh is visible in 'vtysh -c "show running-config"' but absent from 'config list bgp' or the CONFIG_DB BGP_NEIGHBOR table
bgpcfgd log shows 'Generating FRR config' after a CONFIG_DB event, then FRR config reverts to the generated template
Engineer reports 'I fixed the BGP config this morning but it broke again after the container crashed'
what_goes_wrong: In SONiC 202511, bgpcfgd is the authoritative translator between CONFIG_DB and FRR. It watches CONFIG_DB via swsscommon's SubscriberStateTable and generates FRR configuration by rendering Jinja2 templates (in /usr/share/sonic/templates/bgpd.conf.j2 and frr.conf.j2), then calls 'vtysh -f' to apply them. Any configuration written directly via vtysh is not reflected in CONFIG_DB, not persisted to /etc/sonic/config_db.json, and will be silently overwritten the next time bgpcfgd renders and applies its template -- which happens on bgp container restart, 'config reload', or when bgpcfgd processes any CONFIG_DB change event that triggers a full FRR config regeneration.
why_it_breaks: bgpcfgd owns the FRR config. On any CONFIG_DB change event that triggers a template re-render, bgpcfgd calls 'vtysh -f /etc/frr/frr.conf' with the freshly generated config, which replaces the entire FRR running config including any direct vtysh additions.
FRR's own /etc/frr/frr.conf is also overwritten by bgpcfgd renders. If someone saves via 'vtysh -c write', that file will be overwritten on the next bgpcfgd render cycle.

bgp container restarts (e.g., after an OOM kill, a 'systemctl restart bgp', or a 'config reload') fully re-initialize FRR from the bgpcfgd-generated config. Direct vtysh state is not persisted anywhere.

Changes made via vtysh are invisible to 'show running-config' in the SONiC CLI (which reads CONFIG_DB), creating a split-brain where the operator believes their change is in effect but it does not survive the next restart.
